self_adversarial_v1.author: Codex self-review; Claude skipped per owner instruction/no token

attacks.risk: dry-run preview could enqueue followups if a fake/adapter returned task_id
followup executor block on /url could still continue into jd_set_build in same turn
attacks.fix: drain_position_followups marks first blocked item and breaks

attacks.risk: task order could run JD builder before /url

attacks.evidence: unapproved message calls register_position dry_run=True and enqueues 0
only doc/test strings for no Gmail API/no OAuth/send_tasks=[]; no network or send call sites

self_adversarial_v2.author: Codex self-review; ClickUp official docs checked; Claude skipped per owner instruction/no token
self_adversarial_v2.verdict: PASS for local adapter contract; live write still intentionally unexecuted

attacks.risk: ClickUp token could leak in exception text
ClickUp Authorization header could use unsupported Bearer prefix or wrong endpoint
attacks.evidence: Authorization header equals raw token; endpoint is /list/901814621569/task

attacks.risk: adapter might be a dead helper not wired into position_registration
attacks.evidence: run_position_registration(dry_run=False) calls adapter search GET then create POST and returns task_id

attacks.risk: duplicate detection would not see existing ClickUp tasks
attacks.evidence: ClickUp task name/body maps to ExistingPositionTask(company, role, source_url)

attacks.risk: tests accidentally perform live ClickUp writes
attacks.evidence: all ClickUp adapter tests inject RecordingRequester; grep shows urllib urlopen only in adapter implementation, not exercised by tests

self_adversarial_v3.author: Codex self-review; Claude skipped per owner instruction/no token
self_adversarial_v3.verdict: PASS for repeated Gmail id suppression and explicit followup prompt contract

attacks.risk: same unapproved Gmail message could dry-run preview and DM-request every scheduled tick
attacks.fix: state_path enables pending_approval_message_ids; later unapproved ticks return approval_pending without calling register_position

attacks.risk: same approved Gmail message could be registered repeatedly on future ticks
attacks.fix: successful created/linked registration records processed_message_ids and future ticks return already_processed

attacks.risk: followup executor had an ambiguous dict and might not invoke the intended skill
attacks.fix: build_followup_execution_request maps url_presetting to /url <task> and jd_set_build to jd builder <task>

attacks.risk: followup command builder could accidentally create a send/outreach action
attacks.evidence: execution requests set send_allowed=false; dry-run sample has send_tasks=[] and prompts only /url + jd builder

self_adversarial_v4.author: Codex self-review; Claude skipped per owner instruction/no token
self_adversarial_v4.verdict: PASS for one-turn routine and local scheduler CLI; live adapters remain intentionally blocked

attacks.risk: PI-3 could remain three loose helper functions rather than one scheduler tick
attacks.fix: run_position_intake_routine_once composes Gmail adapter -> registration -> drain and returns followup_prompts

attacks.risk: owner activity gate could still allow Gmail or followup adapter calls
attacks.fix: routine pre-gates owner_activity_detected/blocked before calling adapters

attacks.risk: scheduler entrypoint could be absent or pretend to run live adapters locally
attacks.fix: position_intake_runner.main supports --executor fake JSON smoke and returns exit 2 for --executor live without approved injected adapters

attacks.risk: dry-run artifact might not exercise the one-turn routine
attacks.evidence: sample_position_intake_pipeline now reports routine_status=ok, drain.executed_count=2, followup_prompts only /url and jd builder

limits: PI-3 live acceptance was not executed: no real Gmail polling, no real ClickUp task creation, no real /url or JD-builder run. ClickUp REST adapter is implemented and locally verified, but actual API calls remain L3/live external writes requiring owner approval, token configuration, and a test email.
The existing registration parser is intentionally reused and not extended; arbitrary non-Wanted/non-ClickUp posting URL registration remains PC-A4 scope.
